prompt: A wide architectural photograph of a grand brutalist building exterior, dramatic overcast sky, harsh directional side lighting. Multiple distinct staircases visible across the facade. Each individual staircase, traced in isolation left to right, clearly and unambiguously descends. But the entire building structure, seen from leftmost to rightmost edge, unmistakably rises and ascends steeply left to right. Both simultaneously visible and undeniable. Every part goes down. The whole goes up. Raw concrete brutalist architecture, no people. Deep shadow in stair recesses emphasizing the downward direction of each flight. Photorealistic, architectural photography, wide angle lens. No text, no labels, no numbers, no arrows, no diagrams, no annotations. Negative prompt: no text, no labels, no arrows, no signs, no people, no numbers, no diagrams, no floating overlays, no upward-facing staircases individually
visual_logic: A brutalist building where every individual staircase unmistakably descends left to right, yet the whole building structure unmistakably ascends left to right — local and global trends contradicting each other in pure architecture, simultaneously visible and irreconcilable.
